mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading window.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it confirms labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns due to reduced consumer spending expectations and downward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts signals a supply-demand imbalance where manufacturing capital expenditure peaks while residential investment contracts, historically compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When real money supply growth expands while CPI remains sticky above 215, liquidity injections outpace price pressures, directly boosting equity valuations as corporate discount rates stabilize despite inflation persistence.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annually while CPIAUCSL stays above 215.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in real GDP directly expands the corporate earnings base, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as revenue growth outpaces cost inflation across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates exceeding 2% annualized over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k monthly while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, it confirms a labor market inflection that triggers equity risk-off positioning as consumer durables spending collapses.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3m avg change < 100k AND UNRATE rising
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE inflection condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate remains elevated signals that supply-side capacity is expanding faster than monetary policy is tightening, reducing discount rate pressure and supporting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The INDPRO/FEDFUNDS divergence fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of trading windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 750k units while the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5%, it indicates a credit-sensitive demand contraction that historically precedes equity drawdowns as consumer credit cycles tighten.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where both HOUST drops below 750k and FEDFUNDS stays above 0.5%.
